security_deposit.deposit_transfer_on_sale_rule: (2) The landlord shall, within 30 days of receipt of advance rent or a security deposit, give written notice to the tenant which shall include disclosure of the advance rent or deposit, the name and address of the depository or surety company, whether the tenant is entitled to interest, and a statement that reads substantially as follows: 'Your lease requires payment of certain deposits. The landlord may transfer the deposit to any new owner or may return the deposit to you. If the landlord transfers the deposit, the new owner must notify you within 30 days of the transfer.'
security_deposit.special_rules_local_overrides_note: Local ordinances and special housing programs can add stricter rules.

eviction.nonpayment_notice_type: Varies — confirm required notice form (pay-or-quit, demand for possession, etc.).
eviction.lease_violation_notice_type: Varies — confirm notice type and cure rules.
eviction.court_process_summary_rule: Fla. Stat. § 83.56. TERMINATION OF RENTAL AGREEMENT.
eviction.filing_court_rule: Fla. Stat. § 83.56. TERMINATION OF RENTAL AGREEMENT.
eviction.service_of_process_rule: Fla. Stat. § 83.56. TERMINATION OF RENTAL AGREEMENT.

landlord_entry.allowed_entry_reasons_rule: (1) The tenant shall not unreasonably withhold consent to the landlord to enter the dwelling unit from time to time in order to inspect the premises; make necessary or agreed repairs, decorations, alterations, or improvements; supply agreed services; or exhibit the dwelling unit to prospective or actual purchasers, mortgagees, tenants, workers, or contractors. (1) The tenant shall not unreasonably withhold consent to the landlord to enter the dwelling unit from time to time in order to inspect the premises; make necessary or agreed repairs, decorations, alterations, or improvements; supply agreed services; or exhibit the dwelling unit to prospective or actual purchasers, mortgagees, tenants, workers, or contractors.
landlord_entry.penalty_for_illegal_entry_rule: Varies — confirm remedies (injunction, damages, attorney fees) under state law.

rent_increase.cap_rule: No statewide rent control; local rules may apply.
rent_increase.fixed_term_increase_allowed_rule: Varies — confirm your lease terms and statute.
rent_increase.month_to_month_increase_allowed_rule: Varies — generally allowed with proper notice.
rent_increase.delivery_method_rule: Varies — confirm written notice and delivery method requirements.
rent_increase.retaliation_protection_note: Retaliation protections may limit rent increases after protected tenant complaints; confirm local/state rules.

habitability.landlord_repair_duty_rule: (a) Comply with the requirements of applicable building, housing, and health codes; or
habitability.tenant_notice_required_rule: 1. The extermination of rats, mice, roaches, ants, wood-destroying organisms, and bedbugs. When vacation of the premises is required for such extermination, the landlord shall not be liable for damages but shall abate the rent. The landlord must provide reasonable notice of not less than 7 days with a written notice of the need to vacate for a period not to exceed 4 days;
habitability.tenant_termination_for_unrepaired_condit: Varies — some states allow termination after notice and failure to repair.
habitability.code_enforcement_contact_rule: Varies — contact local housing/code enforcement (city/county) for inspections and official repair orders.
